Budget.title: Budget
Budget.description: Represents the overall budget for a celebration, including event details.

name.description: Name of the plan or budget (e.g., 'Elkay's Wedding Plan').

eventType.description: The type of event (e.g., Wedding, Birthday, Funeral).

grandTotal.description: The overall grand total of the budget.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a category within the budget (e.g., 'Venue', 'Catering', 'Decorations').

total.description: The total cost for this category.

order.description: The display order of the category.

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Represents an individual item within a category (e.g., 'Wedding Cake', 'Flowers', 'DJ').

unitPrice.description: Price per unit of the item.

total.description: Total cost of the item (quantity * unitPrice).

comment.description: Comment on how the item cost was determined.

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

MustDo.title: MustDo
MustDo.description: Represents a critical, non-budgeted action item for an event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the must-do item.

title.description: A short, action-oriented title for the task.

note.description: Optional context or details about the task.

status.description: The completion status of the task.

importance.description: The priority level of the task.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the item was created.

reminderEnabled.description: Whether an email reminder is enabled for this task.

reminderDaysBefore.description: How many days before the deadline to send the reminder.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. It leverages path-based ownership for user-specific data and avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies by denormalizing authorization context where necessary. The structure also supports secure list operations via structural segregation.

Budgets, Categories and Items are nested under the `/users/{userId}` collection, ensuring that only the user has access to their own data (Path-Based Ownership).

This structure will need to be implemented with corresponding Firebase Security Rules.
